summary: Fiery Mexicali solo, tight China>Rider, strong Estimated Prophet > Eyes
blurb: The show delivers a scorching Mexicali Blues solo and a seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition. Highlights include New Minglewood Blues, Sugaree, Estimated Prophet into Eyes of the World, and an energetic encore of Brokedown Palace. Overall performance is solid with Brent’s keyboards shining and Jerry’s guitar on fire.
review: December 6, 1981 at Rosemont Horizon captured a Dead in good form as they rolled through a set that balanced blues grit, folk storytelling, and expansive improvisation. The first set opened with a ripping New Minglewood Blues and a soulful Sugaree before diving into a tight run of Mama Tried, Mexicali Blues (where Jerry unleashed a jazzy three‑minute solo), Candyman and the ever‑playful China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider. In the second set, Phil’s bass anchored an inspired Estimated Prophet that seamlessly morphed into an exploratory Eyes of the World, leading into a classic drum/space segment before the band closed with Wharf Rat, Good Lovin’ and a heartfelt Brokedown Palace encore. Brent Mydland settled comfortably behind the kit, adding texture to the Pearl Harbor‑themed space and supporting the group’s dynamic shifts. While a few listeners noted a fleeting “dialogue‑like” tension between Jerry and Bob, the overall vibe remained tight and joyous, making this show a worthwhile listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, praising standout moments such as the Mexicali solo, Estimated Prophet > Eyes jam, and China Cat Sunflower transition; mixed reviews add no consistent negative criticism of the performance itself. The overall sentiment is therefore moderately strong positive.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, distortion‑free audio; includes the full set and showcases the highlighted performances.

key_highlights: Ripping New Minglewood Blues opener and soulful Sugaree
Firey Mexicali Blues solo that puts Jerry on fire
Epic Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World improvisation
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers strong guitar work and a standout Mexicali solo; vocals are solid on the soundboard recordings, though one audience source notes them as distant. A few listeners sensed a brief musical disconnect with Bob.
band_performance.Phil: Provides steady bass foundation and participates in the Pearl Harbor‑themed space alongside Brent, adding depth to the improvisational sections.
band_performance.Bob: Praised as an exceptional rhythm guitarist; his vocal rap is lively though occasionally strained. Some reviewers felt a momentary tension with Jerry but overall contributes solid rhythm chops.
band_performance.Brent: Shows confident keyboard playing, especially in the thematic Pearl Harbor space and song endings; his textures help glue the set together.
